# Marko Stankovic

> Generalist Technical Artist and gameplay programmer based in Serbia, open to
> remote or EU roles. Anchored on tools and pipeline: editor tooling, asset
> pipelines, and automation in Unity and Unreal Engine 5, plus gameplay systems
> and AI state machines. Primary languages C# and C++.

Marko has three years in Unity 3D and is extending into Unreal Engine 5 and the
art-engineering bridge. He builds the systems under a game: AI that thinks,
procedural worlds, and editor tools that get out of the way. He also ships
full-stack web work in TypeScript on Next.js, and is currently building a Claude
Code video studio for short-form developer content on a Remotion backbone. He
works CLI-first on Arch Linux.

## Skills
- Unity 3D, three years, primary engine
- Unreal Engine 5, active, migrating from Unity
- C#, primary language; C++
- TypeScript, Node and Next.js
- Editor tooling, asset pipelines, and automation
- AI state machines, procedural generation, object pooling, event-driven architecture

## Projects
- [DeployLog](https://deploylog.dev): changelog and release-notes platform that
  turns git commits into a published changelog (hosted page, embeddable widget,
  npm CLI, and a GitHub Action) from one source, with AI-drafted notes. Built
  full-stack on Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Status: launching. Details:
  https://markostankovic.org/projects/deploylog
- [BlenderBridge](https://github.com/marko-builds/BlenderBridge): five manual
  cleanup steps on every Blender-to-Unreal import, cut to one click. A UE5 editor
  plugin that enforces asset naming, fixes normal maps, generates lightmap UVs,
  and runs a pre-flight validation pass. Hybrid build: a thin C++ Slate panel over
  a Python core. Open source, v0.1.
- [Tic-Tac-Toe](https://marko-builds.itch.io/tictactoe): shipped local-multiplayer
  Unity 6 game whose real story is a data-driven theme system. Six themes repaint
  board, UI, HUD, and audio from one ScriptableObject and zero new code, split
  across five segregated interfaces. Details:
  https://markostankovic.org/projects/tictactoe
- [Hide & Seek](https://markostankovic.org/projects/hide-and-seek): Unity 6 stealth
  game with NavMesh AI, a six-state generic StateMachine, continuous suspicion
  tracking, and nine custom Level Builder editor tools.

## Writing
- [Devlog](https://markostankovic.org/devlog): build notes on game systems and tooling
- [Replacing a Tangled Update Loop with a Generic State Machine in Unity](https://markostankovic.org/devlog/enemy-ai-state-machine)
- [Fixing GC Allocation Spikes in Unity with a Queue-Based Object Pool](https://markostankovic.org/devlog/object-pooling-endless-runner)
- [One ScriptableObject Per Theme: A Data-Driven Theme System in Unity](https://markostankovic.org/devlog/tictactoe-theme-system)
